project: End-to-End Tests with Lemon Squeezy Test Mode API
description: Add a comprehensive end-to-end test suite for better-auth-lemonsqueezy that runs against the real Lemon Squeezy API in test mode. Tests will validate the full plugin lifecycle — from customer creation through checkout completion (via Playwright) to webhook processing and subscription management — without mocking any external API calls.

userStories.title: E2E Test Infrastructure Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the e2e test infrastructure (server, tunnel, Vitest config) set up, so that I can run e2e tests locally and in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test:e2e starts the test server, tunnel, and runs tests.
.env.e2e.example documents all required env vars.
vitest.config.e2e.ts is configured for sequential, single-process execution.
globalSetup starts server + tunnel; globalTeardown cleans up.

userStories.title: API Smoke Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want smoke tests that verify my LS test store is configured correctly, so that I get a fast failure with a clear message if setup is wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests 1.1–1.7 pass.
Clear error messages if env vars are missing or API key is invalid.

userStories.title: Customer Lifecycle Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that create real LS customers via the plugin, so that I know the customer creation flow works against the real API.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests 2.1–2.3 pass.
Customer exists in both local DB and LS API.

userStories.title: Playwright Checkout Flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright to complete the LS checkout page, so that I can test the full checkout-to-webhook-to-subscription pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests 3.1–3.8 pass.
Checkout page selectors are in e2e/selectors/checkout.ts.
Screenshots are captured on failure.
Test uses card number 4242 4242 4242 4242.

userStories.title: Subscription Management Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that cancel, resume, update, and sync subscriptions against the real API, so that I know these operations work end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests 4.1–4.8 pass.
Each management action is verified via both API response and webhook-triggered DB update.

userStories.title: Webhook Security Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that verify webhook signature validation works correctly, so that I know the endpoint rejects forged requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests 5.1–5.4 pass.

userStories.title: Access Control Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want access control helpers tested against real subscription data, so that I know plan gating works with real LS subscription states.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests 6.1–6.6 pass.

userStories.title: Usage Reporting Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want usage reporting tested against the real API, so that I know metered billing works.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests 7.1–7.3 pass (or suite is skipped if products are not usage-based).

userStories.title: MCP Tool Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every MCP tool tested against the real API, so that I know the MCP server returns valid data.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests 8.1–8.17 pass.
Audit log resource contains entries from previous tool calls.

userStories.title: CI/CD Pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want e2e tests running in GitHub Actions on a schedule, so that I catch LS API changes without manual effort.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/e2e.yml is configured.
Tests run weekly and on manual dispatch.
All secrets are configured in the repo.
Test results are visible in the Actions tab.
